footnotes.text: Jesus twice asserts that this is His last Passover with them on earth, and points out that it will have its perfect fulfillment in the Church triumphant in heaven.

In regard to the words of the consecration of the cup, Matthew and Mark follow one formula, whereas Luke and Paul follow another. The meaning, however, is substantially the same. Which shall be shed for you: in the Vulgate, whereas "Which is being shed for you," is found in the Greek.
The Apostles must be prepared through spiritual weapons to meet all sorts of dangers, trials and hardships.
